What They Make: Camshafts and precision-ground gears for automotive OEMs, hydraulics, defence and oil & gas customers; ~2 million camshafts/gears produced annually
Revenue Band (Estimate): Rs.30-100Cr (estimate; registered as an MSME 'small enterprise' under Udyam, which caps turnover well below Rs.500Cr, so comfortably inside the target band)
Decision Maker: Mr. Abhay Khanapure (Proprietor)
Decision Maker Background: Long-tenured proprietor; company built around precision grinding rather than a single celebrity founder story. No press profile of the proprietor was located.
E1 Evidence: Two in-house manufacturing plants; fits its own components using Landis, Junker and Studer precision grinding machines (company website, product pages).
E2 Evidence: Direct phone, email and two physical plant addresses listed on meritech.in; not an aggregator/portal-only listing.
C3 Evidence: IATF 16949:2016 (automotive-specific quality system, harder to obtain than plain ISO 9001) plus ISO 9001:2015; uses Swiss/German-class grinding machines (Landis, Junker, Studer) -- a real capability moat versus a basic job shop.
C4 Evidence: No advanced degree or ex-corporate pedigree found for the proprietor, but the company has invested in IATF certification and multiple precision grinding lines, showing systems-minded operation rather than pure relationship-selling.
C5 Evidence: Sits in auto-component manufacturing (camshafts/gears), a PLI/Make-in-India eligible category with steady replacement and export demand; not a hyper-growth niche, so scored moderate rather than strong.
C6 Evidence: Company is actively hiring engineers with 10+ years' experience (current job posting found on its careers page) -- one concrete growth signal (Hiring). No facility-expansion or funding news found.
C7 Evidence: Dual certification (ISO 9001:2015 + IATF 16949:2016) across two plants is a meaningfully mature quality system for a company this size.
C8 Evidence: Single named proprietor; no second-generation family member, professional director, or succession signal found in public sources.
Verdict: Worth outreach -- strong technical certification (IATF 16949) is the hook, but confirm true revenue and ownership structure (sole proprietorship can mean concentrated decision-making, which cuts both ways) before investing heavy founder time.
Personalization Hook: IATF 16949:2016 certification (an automotive-specific standard well beyond what most Rs.30-100Cr job shops bother obtaining) suggests the proprietor already thinks in 'systems', which maps directly onto Deep Thought's scientific-execution pitch.
Sources: meritech.in (Home, Quality, Careers pages)
